title: clickable-graph widget params
description: Shared widget module for the `clickable-graph` family: a host <svg> whose elements (nodes, regions, edges) are themselves the interactive controls. No sliders, buttons, or selects — the user clicks / drags a hotspot on the SVG and the readout updates. Data model: an ordered `nodes` array, an optional `edges` array, optional `readoutMap` keyed by node id, and `clickAction ∈ {highlight,toggle,reveal}` describing the UX contract. Because these widgets' JS bodies are highly idiosyncratic (custom painters, transitive-closure highlighting, stereographic projection, drag handling), the driving IIFE is carried as an artifact string in `bodyScript` so `renderScript` can byte-match the legacy inline source. A portable consumer (React, SSR, etc.) can ignore `bodyScript` entirely and render from the structured `nodes` / `edges` / `readoutMap` data.

widgetId.description: DOM id for the outer <div class="widget"> wrapper.

svgId.description: DOM id for the host <svg> element.

svgWidthAttr.description: ARTIFACT. Optional raw width attribute value on the <svg>. When present the <svg> is rendered with width="..."; when absent no width attribute is emitted. Some source widgets include `width="820"` and some omit it — this field preserves each original.

svgHeightAttr.description: ARTIFACT. Optional raw height attribute value on the <svg>. Paired with svgWidthAttr above.

title.description: Display title rendered inside .hd > .ttl and inside the <svg>'s <title> element.

titleTag.description: Tag used for the .ttl header element inside .hd. Default 'div' emits <div class="ttl">; 'span' emits <span class="ttl"> (a layout sub-variant in some legacy topics).

hintTag.description: Tag used for the .hint header element inside .hd. Default 'div'; 'span' pairs with titleTag='span' in the span-header layout sub-variant.

hint.description: Optional short hint rendered inside .hd > .hint. If absent, the hint <div> is omitted.

nodes.description: Portable data: the clickable vertices of the graph. Each node is a spot on the SVG the user can click; `kind` is an advisory role tag a portable consumer can use to style (e.g. 'generic', 'closed', 'sink').

edges.description: Portable data: directed edges between nodes. `curve` is the arrow-bow offset (same convention as drawArrow), `kind` is an advisory role tag (e.g. 'given', 'derived').

initialSelection.description: Optional node id that is pre-selected on mount. Advisory for portable consumers — the artifact bodyScript is responsible for the inline-JS representation of initial state.

clickAction.description: UX contract on node click: 'highlight' permanently highlights the clicked node + its dependency set; 'toggle' flips selection on/off; 'reveal' exposes extra data about the clicked node (e.g. reasoning text).

readoutId.description: Optional DOM id for the <div class="readout"> companion element. If absent, no readout div is rendered.

initialReadoutHtml.description: ARTIFACT. Raw HTML placed inside the readout div on initial render. Preserves inline spans / colors from the legacy source.

readoutMap.description: Portable data: keys are node ids from `nodes[].id` (or synthetic selection keys); values are the readout strings / HTML to display when that node is selected. A portable consumer drives its own renderer from this; the artifact bodyScript may or may not consult it.

bodyScript.description: ARTIFACT. The full body of the driving IIFE — everything between `(function(){` and `})();`. Preserves original comments, whitespace, and inline logic so renderScript can byte-match the legacy inline source. A portable consumer ignores this and reconstructs behavior from nodes/edges/readoutMap/clickAction.

sectionComment.description: ARTIFACT. Optional JS block comment placed at the top of the IIFE (e.g. '=== SECTION 5: Specialization poset ==='). Lets the script byte-match its original source when one is present.
